SEARCH

Enum Class CommandWord

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<CommandWord>
CommandWord
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<CommandWord>, Constable
public enum CommandWord extends Enum<CommandWord>
Enumerates the command words recognized by the game.

Each constant corresponds to a user command verb (for example go or help). UNKNOWN represents an unrecognized command word.

Version:
2026.04.19
Author:
Barnabe Jouanard

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class java.lang.Enum

    Enum.EnumDesc<E extends Enum<E>>

  • Enum Constant Summary

    Enum Constants
    Enum Constant
    Description
    back
    Returns to the previous room, when possible.
    cashin
    Exchanges certain items for money.
    drop
    Drops an item from the player's inventory into the room.
    go
    Moves the player to an adjacent room.
    help
    Prints help text and the list of available commands.
    inventory
    Prints the player's inventory.
    look
    Reprints the current room description.
    name
    Changes the player's displayed name.
    quit
    Quits the game.
    take
    Takes an item from the current room.
    test
    Runs commands from a packaged test file.
    UNKNOWN
    Fallback value used when the command word is not recognized.

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static CommandWord[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.

    • valueOf

      public static CommandWord valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null
